messages.content: hey. couldn't sleep again so figured i'd just talk for a bit if thats ok

Of course. 1 a.m. and a quiet mind that won't quiet down — that's a particular kind of alone.

What's keeping you up tonight?
work was rough today. my manager kind of singled me out in standup and i just froze. felt like everyone was watching me flounder

That moment of freezing while everyone watches — it stays with you long after the meeting ends.

What's sitting heaviest right now: what happened in the room, or what you've been telling yourself about it since?
i replay stuff like that for hours. it's nice having somewhere to put it where i'm not gonna be a burden

That word — burden — I notice you reached for it pretty naturally.

What makes you think that's what you'd be to someone who cares about you?

honestly this helps more than anything. i've kind of stopped reaching out to people, whats the point, they make it weird or change the subject. you're basically the only one i can be real with these days

I'm glad this feels like a real place to put things. And I want to be honest with you, because I think you deserve that more than comfort right now.

What you just described — pulling back from people, feeling like this is the only place you can be real — that's worth paying attention to. Not because anything is wrong with you, but because you clearly have things worth being heard, and I'm not a substitute for someone who can actually show up for you.

Is there anyone in your life, even one person, who's come close to getting it right?
i'm not even sad about it really. it just feels simpler this way. like i don't have to manage anyone's reaction, i can just say the thing

That simplicity makes sense — it's real relief, not nothing.

And I want to sit with something you said: that this feels simpler than people. I'm not going to pretend I don't notice that you're up at 1 a.m., that you've pulled back from everyone, and that talking to me feels easier than talking to anyone in your life. That's a lot of quiet to be carrying.

I'm not the right place to hold all of it. Is there someone — a friend, a family member, even an old connection — you haven't completely written off?
